name: Sp Trainmen
description_short: SP Trainmen Federal Credit Union is a Houston-based federal credit union serving transportation workers with 485 members and $2.85M in assets.
description_long:
SP Trainmen Federal Credit Union was established in 1954 and is headquartered in Houston, Texas. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates under a charter number 9496 and maintains one branch location at 12655 Woodforest Boulevard. The institution serves a specialized membership with a multiple common bond primarily focused on the transportation industry, reflecting its roots in serving rail workers and related professionals.

The credit union offers core banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio includes unsecured loans ($181,038), other loans ($589,230), and maintains total loans of $770,268. They also offer Payday Alternative Loans (PALs), which provide an important low-cost borrowing option for members facing short-term financial needs. The institution maintains $2.85 million in total assets with 485 active members.

SP Trainmen operates as a specialized credit union with membership restricted to individuals with transportation-related employment, creating a tight-knit membership community. The credit union is led by CEO Janice K Gordon and Chairperson Darrin C Godejohn. As a federal credit union, it is not a member of the FHLB and does not carry a low-income credit union designation, but maintains NCUSIF insurance coverage for member deposits.

The credit union has faced recent financial challenges, reporting a net loss of $239,664 in its most recent audit period (as of October 1, 2024). This significant loss, combined with the organization's small asset base and membership size, indicates operational difficulties. Members should be aware that while the credit union provides essential banking services to its niche membership, the institution's financial health may warrant monitoring. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Branch banking services at Houston location
pros: Federally chartered credit union with NCUSIF insurance protecting member deposits
Offers Payday Alternative Loans (PALs) as a lower-cost borrowing option
Serves specialized membership in transportation industry with focused service
Long operational history since 1954 demonstrates institutional longevity
Maintains local branch presence in Houston with direct phone access (713-453-1010)
Member-owned structure means profits benefit account holders rather than external shareholders
cons: Reported net loss of $239,664 in most recent audit period raises financial stability concerns
Very small institution with only 485 members and $2.85M assets limits service capacity and resources
Single branch location restricts physical access for members outside Houston area
Membership restricted to transportation industry employees, excluding general public
No website listed, limiting digital banking convenience and information access
best_for: Transportation industry workers (railroad employees and related professionals) seeking community-based banking
Members seeking Payday Alternative Loans as lower-cost alternatives to payday lenders
Local Houston residents in eligible professions wanting personal banking relationships
Current members maintaining existing accounts due to industry-specific membership restrictions
diagnosis: SP Trainmen Federal Credit Union is best for transportation industry workers in the Houston area seeking personalized banking and low-cost loan alternatives through a member-owned institution. The primary caveat is that the credit union's recent significant net loss and very small asset base ($2.85M) raise questions about long-term financial stability that members should monitor carefully.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
